behavior.business_purpose: The board's conversational front door: a warm, per-project Claude/Codex assistant the solo operator talks to in natural language to ask about the board/codebase/past work and to KICK OFF worker agents, without dropping into MCP/CLI/REST. Unlike fire-and-forget Builders, it keeps one live in-process LLM session per (project, butler) so conversation context survives across turns AND server restarts (persisted resume id).

actors.name: operator
actors.notes: single local user, via ButlerView chat tab (UI)

actors.name: cli-mcp-caller
actors.notes: separate processes (pnpm cli butler ask, MCP ask_butler) that cannot read the in-memory SSE stream — use the synchronous /ask primitive

actors.name: board-event-feed
actors.notes: merge/exit/monitor/approval/workspace-crud emitters inject [system event] turns into the DEFAULT butler

actors.name: agent-questions-recommender
actors.notes: non-route caller that warms+uses the default butler on the service-fallback prompt to produce answer recommendations

entry_points.observable: starts the warm session if cold; 201 + {active:true, sessionId}
pushes a turn into the warm session; streamed via SSE; 409 if busy
synchronous turn (CLI/MCP); buffers text, resolves on result/error or 120s timeout
cancels in-flight turn, session stays warm
stop session + forget resume + clear transcript; state goes inactive
switch auth profile → RESTART session (forget resume)
read/upsert project-scoped butler behavior prompt override
per-butler runtime state | switcher state for all defined butlers
history (capped 50), past-session transcript (allowlisted), current transcript, profiles, slash-command autocomplete
global named-butler persona CRUD, capped at MAX_BUTLERS=4
tabbed chat, voice, history, customize, manage-butlers modal
board events injected as [system event] turns into the warm default session, rate-limited 1/30s
thin HTTP client over the /butler routes
thin HTTP wrappers over the same routes

behaviors.statement: Starting the butler provisions a warm in-process LLM session for the (project, butler); a second ensure returns the existing session (no context fork)
behaviors.observable_outcome: POST /ensure returns 201 {active:true, sessionId}; GET /butler then shows active:true; cold session starts WITHOUT a resume when no id persisted

behaviors.statement: Sending a message pushes a turn into the warm session and streams an assistant reply back (token deltas + tool cards) over SSE
behaviors.observable_outcome: user bubble appears, mock/assistant reply streams into chat; /message accepted; reply text returned

behaviors.statement: A synchronous /ask turn lets a separate process (CLI/MCP) get a buffered answer without reading the in-memory SSE stream
behaviors.observable_outcome: POST /ask returns {isError:false, text} containing the assistant reply; resolves on result/error or 120s timeout

behaviors.statement: Conversation context persists across turns: the transcript accumulates user+assistant messages and multiple turns keep prior history
behaviors.observable_outcome: GET /messages returns ordered user+assistant messages; two turns yield 4 messages with both prompts present

behaviors.statement: Clearing context (DELETE /butler) stops the session, forgets the resume id, and empties the transcript — the mechanism for 'new session' / applying an edited skill
behaviors.observable_outcome: GET /butler shows active:false; GET /messages returns []; UI shows 'Butler is ready.' placeholder

behaviors.statement: Interrupting cancels the IN-FLIGHT turn (query.interrupt) while keeping the session warm
behaviors.observable_outcome: in-flight stream stops; GET /butler still active:true; next turn accepted

behaviors.statement: Switching model applies live via the SDK setModel control request and PRESERVES conversation context (no restart); the model lives on the global butler definition
behaviors.observable_outcome: POST /model → {ok, model, applied:true}; SDK setModel called with the alias; GET /butler reflects new model; sessionId unchanged

behaviors.statement: Switching profile (auth endpoint) RESTARTS the session and forgets the old resume id — a different endpoint cannot resume the same transcript
behaviors.observable_outcome: POST /profile → {ok, profile, active:true}; a fresh SDK query starts WITHOUT resume; persisted session-id pref now tracks the new session

behaviors.statement: A persisted SDK session id is replayed as resume on next ensure, so the butler survives a server restart with its conversation intact
preconditions: a session id persisted to butler_session_<projectId>
behaviors.observable_outcome: cold GET /butler reports the persisted sessionId; ensure passes options.resume = that id instead of starting a new conversation

behaviors.statement: A dead/unresumable session (Anthropic invalid-thinking-signature 400, or codex 'no rollout found') is recovered: drop the resume, start fresh, and re-send the in-flight turn so the user's message is not lost
behaviors.observable_outcome: first query throws → second query starts WITHOUT resume; session adopts the fresh id; turn succeeds; no error bubble; genuine (non-stale) failures still surface and do NOT retry

behaviors.statement: A turn is rejected while the butler is busy — a single in-flight turn per session, no concurrent streams into one context
behaviors.observable_outcome: POST /message returns 409 (HTTP); at the service layer sendButlerTurn returns false for the second concurrent turn

behaviors.statement: Named butler personas are GLOBAL (shared across projects), CRUD-managed and capped at MAX_BUTLERS=4; the 'default' butler can be renamed/re-modelled but never deleted (it holds back-compat pref keys)
preconditions: none for list; <4 defs to create
behaviors.observable_outcome: POST creates (201) and appears in list; PUT renames; DELETE removes a named one; default always present; a 5th create is refused; deleting default is refused

behaviors.statement: The butler resolves its backend (claude SDK / codex CLI / mock) via the shared provider cascade; a codex license-profile butler launches under its own CODEX_HOME with --profile dropped
behaviors.observable_outcome: GET /butler reports backend matching the provider; GET /profiles lists provider profiles + selected; a codex license butler spawns with CODEX_HOME=licenseDir and no --profile; claude model aliases not applied to a codex butler

behaviors.statement: A project-scoped butler behavior prompt override can be set and retrieved; it wins over the global default and is scoped per project (no leak); deleting reverts to the global
behaviors.observable_outcome: PUT /skill stores prompt; GET /skill returns it with isOverride:true; another project keeps its own; empty PUT reverts to global default

behaviors.statement: The pure client reducer folds the SSE ButlerEvent stream into chat bubbles + collapsible tool cards deterministically (dedupes user echoes, accumulates assistant deltas, settles tool results, never double-renders streamed text)
behaviors.observable_outcome: given a sequence of events the chat state contains the expected bubbles/tool cards; input state is never mutated

behaviors.statement: Slash-command autocomplete: typing '/' offers commands merged from the live SDK supportedCommands and the repo's own .claude/skills, parsed/filtered/applied client-side
behaviors.observable_outcome: GET /commands returns the merged deduped command list; client parse/filter/apply transform the input correctly

behaviors.statement: Board events (merge failures, agent crashes, stuck workspaces) are injected as [system event] turns into the warm DEFAULT butler only, rate-limited to 1 turn / 30s / project with bursts collapsed to a summary line; dropped silently if no session is warm
behaviors.observable_outcome: a [system event] turn appears in the default butler's transcript; named butlers never receive it; ≥2 events in 30s collapse to one 'Nx kind' summary; cold session → event dropped

behaviors.statement: Past-session history is browsable (capped at 50 ids, most-recent-first) and a past session's transcript is readable ONLY if its id is allowlisted to this project's tracked ids (security boundary)
behaviors.observable_outcome: GET /sessions returns the capped list; GET /sessions/:sid/messages returns the transcript for an own id but REFUSES a session id belonging to another project

behaviors.statement: The 'Chat about this ticket' retrospective builds an opening prompt referencing the ticket by number (or title), its spec questions, type/status, and a truncated description
behaviors.observable_outcome: the generated prompt contains the #number, retrospective questions, metadata, and a truncated description (omitted when empty)

permissions.notes: single-user local app — no RBAC; permission axis is near-trivial EXCEPT the cross-project session-transcript allowlist (butler.history.access), which is a real security boundary worth a deny test

error_states.statement: invalid-thinking-signature/no-rollout resume recovered to a fresh session; genuine failures surfaced and not retried
synchronous /ask resolves with an error after a 120s timeout
reading a past-session transcript for a session id not allowlisted to this project is refused

external_integrations: codex CLI subprocess + CODEX_HOME license ring

unknowns.question: Does POST /message actually return HTTP 409 (vs 200 + ignore) when the session is busy? Only the service-layer boolean is asserted in tests; the HTTP status was not exercised live (read-only, no mutation allowed).
unknowns.why_unknown: not asserted by any test; not probed live to avoid spawning a session

unknowns.question: Is the MAX_BUTLERS=4 cap actually enforced with a refusal on the 5th create, and is deleting 'default' refused?
unknowns.why_unknown: no test creates a 5th or deletes default; not probed live

unknowns.question: Does the event feed's 30s rate-limit / burst-collapse and default-only routing behave as documented at runtime?
unknowns.why_unknown: no test imports/exercises emitButlerSystemEvent's injection path; usage-limit-rotation-exit only imports the module for an unrelated builder-rotation test

unknowns.question: Does the cross-project session-transcript allowlist actually reject a foreign id (403/404)?
unknowns.why_unknown: no negative-path test; not probed live

unknowns.question: Does the recommender path (service-fallback prompt) really diverge in observable behaviour from the chat path?
unknowns.why_unknown: documented as a real split but no behavioural assertion anywhere

covered_by.test: packages/e2e/tests/ui/butler.test.ts::POST /butler/ensure starts a mock session
packages/server/src/__tests__/butler-provider.test.ts::starts cold Butler sessions fresh when no resume id is persisted
packages/e2e/tests/ui/butler.test.ts::sends a message and shows mock response in chat
packages/e2e/tests/ui/butler.test.ts::Start Butler button starts a mock session and reveals chat UI

gap.rationale: happy-path streamed reply asserted under the MOCK backend only; real-provider streaming and mid-turn errors not asserted

covered_by.test: packages/e2e/tests/ui/butler.test.ts::POST /butler/ask returns a mock response
packages/mcp-server/src/__tests__/tools/butler-tools.test.ts::butler_state GETs the butler endpoint and returns the JSON

gap.rationale: success path asserted; the 120s-timeout error resolution path is never exercised

covered_by.test: packages/e2e/tests/ui/butler.test.ts::GET /butler/messages returns transcript after ask
packages/e2e/tests/ui/butler.test.ts::multiple turns maintain conversation history
packages/e2e/tests/ui/butler.test.ts::DELETE /butler clears session and transcript
packages/e2e/tests/ui/butler.test.ts::Clear context button resets the chat
packages/e2e/tests/ui/butler.test.ts::POST /butler/interrupt stops in-flight turn gracefully
packages/mcp-server/src/__tests__/tools/butler-tools.test.ts::butler_interrupt POSTs /interrupt and returns the JSON

gap.rationale: the e2e test interrupts with NO active turn and only asserts res.ok(); the MCP test only checks request shape. The real behaviour — cancelling an IN-FLIGHT stream while keeping the session warm and accepting a subsequent turn — is never asserted

covered_by.test: packages/server/src/__tests__/butler-provider.test.ts::keeps displayed and running model/profile selections consistent across switches
packages/server/src/__tests__/butler-provider.test.ts::keeps displayed and running model/profile selections consistent across switches
packages/server/src/__tests__/butler-sdk-resume-regression.test.ts::forces a fresh session start when profile changes mid-session
packages/server/src/__tests__/butler-provider.test.ts::resumes a persisted Butler session id instead of creating a new conversation
packages/server/src/__tests__/butler-sdk-resume-regression.test.ts::recovers from 'Invalid signature in thinking block' on resume by starting a fresh session
packages/server/src/__tests__/butler-sdk-resume-regression.test.ts::accepts a turn on the fresh session after signature-error recovery
packages/server/src/__tests__/butler-codex-resume-recovery.test.ts::drops a dead resume id and retries the turn on a fresh thread
packages/server/src/__tests__/butler-codex-resume-recovery.test.ts::surfaces a non-resume failure as an error (no infinite retry)
packages/server/src/__tests__/butler-loop-classify.test.ts::resume-reset on invalid thinking signature (only when resuming)
packages/server/src/__tests__/butler-provider.test.ts::rejects overlapping Codex Butler turns instead of enqueueing ambiguous prompts

gap.rationale: only the service-layer sendButlerTurn=false is asserted; the HTTP-level 409 on POST /message while busy is never asserted by any test

covered_by.test: packages/e2e/tests/ui/butler.test.ts::POST then DELETE /api/butler-definitions creates and removes named butler
packages/e2e/tests/ui/butler.test.ts::PUT /api/butler-definitions/:id updates butler name
packages/e2e/tests/ui/butler.test.ts::GET /api/butler-definitions lists definitions including default

gap.rationale: CRUD happy paths asserted, but the two invariants — MAX_BUTLERS=4 cap refusing a 5th create, and 'default' being un-deletable — are never asserted

covered_by.test: packages/server/src/__tests__/butler-provider.test.ts::uses Codex profiles and starts a logical Codex Butler when provider is Codex
packages/server/src/__tests__/butler-provider.test.ts::does not apply saved Claude model aliases to a Codex Butler
packages/server/src/__tests__/butler-codex-home.test.ts::launches a license-profile butler under its CODEX_HOME and drops --profile
packages/server/src/__tests__/butler-codex-home.test.ts::does NOT override CODEX_HOME for a codex butler on the default profile
packages/e2e/tests/ui/butler.test.ts::PUT /butler/skill sets and GET retrieves project-scoped prompt override
packages/server/src/__tests__/butler-skill-repository.test.ts::a project override wins over the global
packages/server/src/__tests__/butler-skill-repository.test.ts::override is scoped per project (one project's override does not leak to another)
packages/client/src/lib/butler-event-reducer.test.ts::reduceButlerEvent — streamed assistant text accumulates deltas
packages/client/src/lib/butler-event-reducer.test.ts::tool-result settles the matching pending tool by id
packages/client/src/lib/butler-event-reducer.test.ts::result does NOT duplicate text already streamed (textSeen=true)
packages/client/src/lib/butler-event-reducer.test.ts::never mutates the input state or buffer
packages/client/src/lib/butler-slash-commands.test.ts::filterCommands filters case-insensitively and caps at 8
packages/client/src/lib/butler-slash-commands.test.ts::applyCommandToInput replaces a trailing slash token

gap.rationale: client parse/filter/apply asserted, but the server-side merge of live SDK supportedCommands with scanned .claude/skills (GET /commands) is not asserted by a test

no test exercises emitButlerSystemEvent's injection path. usage-limit-rotation-exit.test.ts imports butler-event-feed but tests builder license-rotation relaunch, NOT event injection. The 30s rate-limit, burst-collapse-to-summary, default-only routing, and drop-when-cold behaviours are entirely unverified

covered_by.test: packages/e2e/tests/ui/butler.test.ts::GET /butler/sessions returns empty list when no tracked sessions
packages/e2e/tests/ui/butler.test.ts::History panel opens and shows no sessions for a fresh mock session

gap.rationale: the sessions list is only checked for being an array; the 50-id cap is never asserted, and the SECURITY allowlist (a foreign project's session id must be refused on /sessions/:sid/messages) has no negative-path test

covered_by.test: packages/shared/__tests__/butler-ticket-prompt.test.ts::references the ticket by number when available
packages/shared/__tests__/butler-ticket-prompt.test.ts::includes the description but truncates a very long one

top_gaps.rationale: The entire board-event → butler injection pipeline (AK-75) is unverified — no test touches emitButlerSystemEvent. Asserting that a warm default butler receives a [system event] turn, that bursts collapse to one summary within the 30s window, that named butlers DON'T receive it, and that a cold session drops the event would lock four documented invariants at once. Medium exec/maint cost (in-process service test).

The single-in-flight-turn invariant is only asserted at the service-boolean layer; the HTTP 409 on POST /message while busy — the contract the UI and CLI/MCP depend on — is never asserted. A concurrency-sensitive route that should fail closed. Cheap to add via the test-app route harness.

Security boundary: a past-session transcript must be readable ONLY for ids allowlisted to this project (routes/butler.ts:664-667). The deny path (foreign project's session id → refused) has no test, and the 50-id history cap is unasserted. Negative-path test, low maintenance.

Existing tests interrupt with no active turn (touches-only). Need a test that starts a streaming turn, interrupts mid-flight, and asserts the stream stops AND the session stays warm and accepts a follow-up — the actual behaviour users rely on.
Assert the two persona invariants: a 5th create is refused (MAX_BUTLERS=4) and deleting 'default' is refused. Cheap API-level boundary tests guarding documented rules.
